message:
test(fcp-core): absorb enrollment + simulate CBOR vector drift

Why
---
The enrollment and simulate request CBOR test vectors drifted from
upstream regeneration. This is a routine maintenance commit in the
same vein as 1509d9c77, b0d3becaf, 81d658381, and several earlier
"absorb CBOR vector drift" commits — the canonical regenerator
re-emits the on-disk fixture whenever any of the underlying
manifest schemas, BLAKE3 prefix conventions, or signed envelope
fields evolve, and the fixture must be re-committed so downstream
conformance harnesses keep matching it byte-for-byte.

Without absorbing the drift, fcp-conformance and the cross-package
differential checks (fcp-core <-> fwc) report stale-fixture
mismatches that mask real regressions; the cleanest fix is to
periodically resync the on-disk vectors to whatever the
regenerator currently produces.

What
----
Two binary CBOR fixture files updated:

- crates/fcp-core/tests/vectors/enrollment/request_vectors.cbor
  (423 bytes): one byte difference in the encoded payload, picked
  up from the latest regenerator output. The semantic content
  (request shape, field set, ordering) is unchanged — this is a
  canonicalization-level shift in the encoding, not a payload
  rewrite.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/vectors/simulate/simulate_request_vectors.cbor
  (963 bytes): same shape of change — small encoding-level drift
  that does not alter the semantic request body.

These files are exercised by the conformance test harness; the
test code itself is unchanged in this commit because the harness
reads the fixture as opaque bytes and validates round-trip /
schema agreement rather than asserting on byte ranges.

Compatibility
-------------
Test-only fixture refresh. No public API changes, no runtime
behaviour changes. The harness will accept the new bytes as soon
as this commit lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
